markets.question: Spread: Boyacá Chicó FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for May 1 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Boyacá Chicó FC" if Boyacá Chicó FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Llaneros FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on dimayor.com.co. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Boyacá Chicó FC vs. Llaneros FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for May 1 at 7:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boyacá Chicó FC, languishing in 19th place in the Liga DIMAYOR Apertura table amid relegation pressure, hosts 10th-placed Llaneros FC at high-altitude Estadio La Independencia in Tunja on May 2. Chicó's mixed home form includes three wins but recent struggles, while Llaneros boast solid away results with three victories and sit comfortably mid-table after a 2-1 win over Chicó in July 2025. Head-to-head battles remain tightly contested, with eight Chicó wins, seven for Llaneros, and ten draws across 25 meetings, often low-scoring. No major injuries or suspensions reported recently; altitude advantage and table stakes could fuel defensive setups, impacting over/under and both teams to score props.

markets.question: Will Hamas agree to disarm by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hamas officially announces it will disarm in the Gaza Strip by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only credible announcements, for example by the widely acknowledged leadership of Hamas, will qualify.

For the purposes of this market, "disarm" refers to a public commitment to relinquish or dismantle its military, whether partially or completely, in the Gaza Strip.

Announcements of partial disarmament (e.g., surrendering a class of weapons or agreeing to disarm in stages or a certain region) will qualify as long as it is part of an acknowledged disarmament process.

Only official announcements will qualify. Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.

Primary resolution sources will include official statements from Hamas leadership; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted will also qualify.

events.title: Will Hamas agree to disarm by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hamas officially announces it will disarm in the Gaza Strip by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only credible announcements, for example by the widely acknowledged leadership of Hamas, will qualify.

For the purposes of this market, "disarm" refers to a public commitment to relinquish or dismantle its military, whether partially or completely, in the Gaza Strip.

Announcements of partial disarmament (e.g., surrendering a class of weapons or agreeing to disarm in stages or a certain region) will qualify as long as it is part of an acknowledged disarmament process.

Only official announcements will qualify. Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.

Primary resolution sources will include official statements from Hamas leadership; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hamas's repeated rejections of US-backed disarmament proposals from the Board of Peace have stalled phase two of the Gaza ceasefire agreement, with the group demanding full Israeli troop withdrawal and an end to alleged violations before surrendering weapons. In mid-April 2026, direct US-Hamas talks and a New York Times report indicated limited concessions, such as handing over some rifles, but officials dismissed full disarmament as a "trap" undermining Palestinian security. This follows earlier ultimatums and Cairo negotiations, where reconstruction aid hinges on Hamas decommissioning arms, tunnels, and militias. Traders watch upcoming mediator sessions in Doha or Cairo for breakthroughs, amid threats of renewed IDF operations if talks collapse by late May.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be $1.5T or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no OpenAI initial public offering by December 31, 2026, at a 73.5% implied probability, driven by recent reports of internal tensions and execution risks highlighted by CFO Sarah Friar, who has privately flagged the Q4 timeline as overly aggressive amid missed revenue targets, slowing growth, and projected $600 billion in compute spending over five years. Despite a landmark $122 billion funding round in late March closing at an $852 billion post-money valuation—bolstered by backers like SoftBank, Amazon, and Nvidia—secondary market demand for OpenAI shares has softened as investors pivot to rival Anthropic, underscoring sustainability concerns for the AI leader's massive burn rate. CEO Sam Altman's push for a pre-Anthropic IPO has led to Friar's exclusion from key investor meetings on server procurement, amplifying doubts; watch for S-1 filing signals, though a mid-2027 debut now appears more plausible given organizational hurdles and unproven cash flow paths.

markets.question: Santos: Valerio Aboian vs Pedro Sakamoto
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Valerio Aboian and Pedro Sakamoto in the Santos, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Valerio Aboian' if Valerio Aboian advances against Pedro Sakamoto.

This market will resolve to 'Pedro Sakamoto' if Pedro Sakamoto advances against Valerio Aboian.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Valerio Aboian at 50% implied probability for their ATP Challenger Santos round-of-32 clash on clay, capturing the competitive balance between the No. 452-ranked Argentine and No. 424 Brazilian Pedro Sakamoto, who holds a 1-0 head-to-head edge from a grueling three-set win (6-7, 7-6, 6-4) last June in Santa Cruz. Both arrive off recent clay-court losses—Sakamoto 1-2 to Juan Bautista Torres on April 15 and Aboian 0-2 to Hugo Dellien on April 17—amid middling 2026 records of 17-13 and 7-10, respectively. Sakamoto's home-crowd boost in Santos could tip scales, while Aboian's endurance in long rallies poses upset threat; no injury concerns reported, but weather or late scratches remain variables.

markets.question: Will "Bully - Ye" be the Billboard 200 #1 album for the week of May 9?
markets.description:
Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then dated “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”.

This market will resolve according to the number 1 album on the Billboard 200 chart dated “Week of May 9, 2026”.

This market will resolve as soon as the relevant chart is published. If the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week is not published within 14 calendar days of the expected release date, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week, published on the Billboard website (https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/) or through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy entered the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinals with a perfect 2-0 Group A record, showcasing strong map control on Inferno, Dust II, and Ancient against LPH Gaming and Sangal ALTERS. INFINITE, seeded from Group D's 2-1 runner-up finish, brought momentum from their United21 League Season 46 grand final win over BIG Academy but faced stand-in fnl replacing Blytz on map one amid roster tweaks including April additions like Dytor and sl3nd. Higher HLTV-ranked (#65 vs. #224) INFINITE were external favorites, yet Imperial's stable lineup since March—featuring ta9z, Kragh, and sh1Fu—delivered a 2-1 BO3 upset via dominant Ancient and Nuke performances in the April 9 double-elimination matchup.

markets.question: Will Trump meet with Vladimir Putin in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual meets with Donald Trump between May 1, and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both the listed individual and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Who will Trump meet with in May?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual meets with Donald Trump between May 1, and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both the listed individual and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump is confirmed to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 14-15, a summit rescheduled from April amid escalating US-Iran tensions and aimed at stabilizing bilateral trade relations, including concerns over Chinese vehicles entering via Mexico and Canada. Official White House schedules through May 3 show only domestic events like intelligence briefings, a seniors gathering in Florida, and a PGA Tour appearance, with no other bilateral meetings announced. Traders weigh this locked-in diplomacy—Xi's first such visit since 2017—against potential unconfirmed talks, such as speculation around Russian President Putin's separate China trip, as geopolitical deadlines like Iran policy reviews loom before month-end resolution.

markets.question: Will CZ post 100-119 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Changpeng Zhao (@cz_binance), Binance founder and crypto influencer, maintains steady X posting activity focused on industry insights, BNB Chain developments, book promotions for his memoir "Freedom of Money" (proceeds to charity), and replies to followers. Prior weekly markets on Polymarket resolved to the 20-39 posts range amid consistent cadence in April 2026, reflecting trader consensus on his reliable engagement post-prison pardon. Recent posts include May 2 book plugs and April reactions to ETF launches and scams, with a Dubai signing event upcoming. Absent shifts like reduced visibility or major announcements, patterns suggest sustained volume through May 5-12.

markets.question: Will UK GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 1.5% and 1.8%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the UK real gross domestic product (GDP) growth rate (% change) for Q1 of 2026 compared with the same quarter a year ago in the "GDP First quarterly estimate, UK" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/bulletins/gdpfirstquarterlyestimateuk/previousreleases

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign 51.6% implied probability to 0.6-0.9% quarter-on-quarter UK GDP growth for Q1 2026, reflecting resilient services sector expansion (+0.2% in three months to January per ONS monthly data) and February's 0.8% year-on-year three-month advance, which offset construction contraction (-2.0%) and bolstered nowcasts around 0.4-0.7% from Pantheon Macroeconomics and similar forecasters. Sticky March CPI inflation at 3.3%—above the Bank of England 2% target—prompted the MPC to hold Bank Rate at 3.75% on April 29, tempering growth expectations and elevating the 30.5% odds on 0.3-0.6%, amid Middle East tensions hiking energy costs and OECD/IMF annual 2026 downgrades to 0.7-0.8%. The ONS preliminary Q1 release, due May 14, remains the key catalyst for resolution.

markets.question: Will Amanda Anisimova be the 2026 Women’s Wimbledon Winner?

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aryna Sabalenka tops trader consensus at 29% implied probability for the 2026 Wimbledon women's singles title, bolstered by her sustained world No. 1 WTA ranking and near-perfect 26-2 record through early clay events despite a recent Madrid Open upset to Hailey Baptiste and prior Stuttgart withdrawal. Elena Rybakina sits close at 21%, leveraging her 2022 Championships win, flat serve prowess on grass, and fresh Porsche Tennis Grand Prix clay crown on April 19. Iga Świątek lags at 15.7% amid a shaky 13-7 season marked by upsets like Miami's loss to Magda Linette, exposing her grass vulnerabilities. The tight top bunch underscores grass-court volatility—quick points and big servers thrive, yet depth from 2023 champion Markéta Vondroušová (5%), Amanda Anisimova's resurgence (6.1%), and untested pre-Wimbledon tune-ups like Nottingham keep the field wide open.

markets.question: Palantir total customers above 1020 in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Palantir’s total customer count (typically labeled "customer count") for the first fiscal quarter of 2026, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 

If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Palantir’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

events.title: Palantir # of customers above __ in Q1?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Palantir’s total customer count (typically labeled "customer count") for the first fiscal quarter of 2026, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 

If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Palantir’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) approaches its Q1 2026 earnings release on May 4, with total customer count serving as a pivotal metric tracking Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) adoption and U.S. commercial expansion. Q4 2025 reported 954 customers, up 34% year-over-year and 5% sequentially, alongside 137% U.S. commercial revenue growth and FY2026 guidance for 61% total revenue increase to $7.2 billion, including 115% U.S. commercial acceleration. Consensus forecasts Q1 revenue at $1.54 billion (74% YoY) and adjusted EPS of $0.28 (115% YoY), reflecting sustained demand despite stock's 14% YTD decline amid 217x forward P/E valuation. Options market prices 10.5% implied volatility for post-earnings reaction.

markets.question: Will Adam Miller win the 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Los Angeles mayoral election will be held on June 2, 2026, to elect the mayor of Los Angeles, California. If no candidate receives a majority of the vote, a runoff election will be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins the election.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the City of Los Angeles.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely watched Los Angeles mayoral nonpartisan primary on June 2, trader consensus prices City Councilmember Nithya Raman as the frontrunner at 56.5%, driven by her late-March Loyola Marymount University poll lead over incumbent Mayor Karen Bass and strong fundraising that surpassed the mayor's totals reported April 25. Bass trails at 22% amid high unfavorables from a March LA Times survey and persistent challenges on homelessness and public safety, while reality TV personality Spencer Pratt's 20% reflects his top fundraising and outsider appeal capturing protest votes. A UCLA Luskin poll showing 40% undecided underscores volatility, with recent social media efforts seeking to block Raman by boosting Pratt in this top-two contest.

markets.question: Will ForecastEx self-certify sports event contracts by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects uncertainty around which Designated Contract Markets (DCMs) will self-certify sports event contracts by the June 30, 2026 deadline, following the CFTC's pivotal March 12 advisory clarifying compliance pathways. The guidance urges DCMs to engage sports leagues pre-certification, use official data for settlement to mitigate manipulation risks, and detail economic purpose—prompting partnerships like the CFTC-MLB MOU on baseball integrity. A Third Circuit ruling on April 6 affirmed exclusive CFTC jurisdiction over DCM-listed sports contracts, preempting state gambling laws and easing legal hurdles. No new sports self-certifications have emerged in recent weeks amid ongoing rulemaking comments, leaving odds closely contested for challengers like Railbird and Aristotle while majors like CME lead decisively. Heightened scrutiny and prop bet concerns could delay others.

markets.question: Will Oliver Adams Larkin be the Democratic Nominee for FL-23?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the FL-23 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on August 18, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Jared Moskowitz holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the FL-23 Democratic primary on August 18, but challenger Oliver Larkin has narrowed the gap through progressive endorsements from DSA, Progressive Change Campaign Committee, and local socialist groups, capitalizing on voter concerns over Moskowitz's pro-Israel stance and opposition to an Iran war powers resolution. A March Center for Strategic Politics poll showed Moskowitz leading 45%-11% initially, but Larkin surged to a 49%-36% advantage after balanced candidate biographies, highlighting persuadable Democratic primary voters on foreign policy and economic issues like Medicare for All. The race remains tight due to Moskowitz's fundraising strength and name recognition versus Larkin's grassroots momentum; debates or new polls could tip the balance in Florida's closed primary.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 280-299 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping purge Wang Huning in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if either of the following conditions are met by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

1) The listed individual is removed or resigns from their primary political post or from their position, if any, on the Chinese Politburo or its standing committee, with a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor.

2) The listed individual is expelled from the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). 

Qualifying announcements and reporting of a purge before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal/expulsion goes into effect.

A listed individual leaving office at the end of a regularly scheduled term, or being removed/resigning from office without a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor, will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from the Chinese Government and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's ongoing anti-corruption campaign has driven a sweeping purge of senior People's Liberation Army (PLA) officers in 2026, with Central Military Commission (CMC) vice chairs Zhang Youxia and Liu Zhenli removed in January for disciplinary violations, followed by the ouster of nine military delegates from the National People's Congress in late February and three retired generals in early March ahead of the Two Sessions. These actions, emphasizing political loyalty amid military modernization, have created leadership vacancies across rocket forces, navy, and theater commands, continuing a pattern since 2022. No major civilian or Politburo-level purges reported since March, though traders monitor for escalations before the 21st Party Congress in 2027.

markets.question: Will xAI have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

Watch for late-May leaderboard shifts or pre-June model tweaks as pivotal catalysts.

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will the official USD to ARS exchange rate be between 1450.00 and 1499.99 at the end of December 2026?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentina’s official wholesale U.S. dollar exchange rate at market close on the last business day of December 2026, as published by the Central Bank of Argentina (BCRA).

This market will resolve according to the official wholesale USD to ARS exchange rate (Tipo de Cambio Mayorista, ($ por USD) Com. A 3500 | Referencia) at market close on the final business day of December 2026, as published by the BCRA on its official website (https://www.bcra.gob.ar/).

If the official rate for that date has not been published by the end of the 7th day after the end of the specified month, the market will resolve according to the most recently published official wholesale rate preceding that date.

The resolution source for this market will be the official BCRA publication. Resolution will occur once this figure is available.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the Central Bank of Argentina (BCRA), which reports the Wholesale Exchange Rate to two decimal points (e.g., 1,408.02 ARS per USD). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' consensus, backed by real capital, prices a 39.5% implied probability for Argentina's official wholesale USD/ARS exchange rate exceeding 1600 by December 31, 2026, with 22.4% odds for 1500–1549.99, reflecting expectations of controlled peso depreciation amid President Milei's disinflation campaign, which slashed annual inflation to around 31.5% by end-2025 from triple digits. Current rate hovers near 1393 ARS/USD as of early May 2026, stabilized after a late-April spike to 1440 amid thin net reserves under $3 billion and external debt pressures. Recent Central Bank easing of FX controls in April supports reserve-building but highlights intervention reliance in the crawling peg regime, with mid-term elections and potential policy tweaks as key catalysts ahead.

markets.question: Will Jasmine Paolini win the 2026 Women’s French Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Aryna Sabalenka at 31.5% implied probability for the 2026 Roland Garros title, driven by her dominant 25-1 record this season, world No. 1 ranking, and strong Madrid Open performance—including a deep run with ruthless aggression on clay, extending her streak to 14 matches amid a 19-1 record there since 2023. Iga Świątek's odds at 16.5% reflect concerns over her mid-match retirement in Madrid due to a virus causing dizziness and poor physical form, disrupting clay preparation despite her surface dominance. Elena Rybakina (13.4%) holds as a top contender with multiple 2026 titles and top-10 wins, while Coco Gauff (9.0%) benefits from defending champion status; Mirra Andreeva (7.5%) surges on Madrid final momentum against winner Marta Kostyuk (3.4%), highlighting next-gen upside ahead of Rome and the Grand Slam draw.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Wolverhampton Wanderers and Burnley already mathematically relegated to the Championship, Nottingham Forest (39 points from 34 games), Tottenham Hotspur (37 points from 35), and West Ham United (36 points from 35) are locked in a tense Premier League relegation scrap with three matches remaining. West Ham's 3-0 defeat to Brentford on May 2 exposed defensive frailties, dropping them into the drop zone despite a prior win streak, while Spurs' recent 1-0 victory over Wolves provided brief respite but left them vulnerable after a winless 2026 start in the league until late April. Forest's surge—three wins in their last four—bolsters their five-point buffer, though all face daunting run-ins against Chelsea, Arsenal, Newcastle, and others; goal difference (-4 Forest, -9 Spurs, -19 West Ham) could prove decisive in tight finishes.

markets.question: Will the UK’s 2026 inflation be at least 4.5%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the U.K. over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Office for National Statistics (ONS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly ONS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the ONS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases), currently scheduled to be released on January 20, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by choosing the report for the relevant month from the previous releases page for price indices on the ONS website (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases). The relevant figure can be found in the "Consumer price inflation rates" table under the column labeled "CPI 12-month rate (%)" for the relevant month.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly ONS CPI news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.ons.gov.uk/releasecalendar

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 45.5% implied probability for U.K. annual CPI inflation reaching 4.5% or higher in 2026, reflecting March 2026's unexpected rise to 3.3% from 3.0%, driven by sharp motor fuel increases (petrol up 8.6 pence per litre) and heating oil surges amid Middle East conflict-elevated energy prices, with Brent crude at $119 per barrel. Services inflation hit 4.5%, core CPI eased slightly to 3.1%, but Bank of England scenarios project peaks over 3.5% by year-end, potentially 6%+ under prolonged shocks, as indirect effects build in food and supply chains. MPC held Bank Rate at 3.75% on April 29 (8-1 vote), signaling vigilance on second-round risks; key catalysts include April CPI release on May 20 and June 18 MPC meeting.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 7°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in forecast models for Ankara's May 5 high temperature, with the Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) projecting a maximum of 13°C under cloudy skies, low of 2°C, and light winds around 9 km/h, while ensemble spreads from ECMWF and GFS show slight divergence toward 12°C amid persistent cool northerly flow and high humidity (55-96%). Today's observed chill—high near 8°C with morning showers—reinforces the cold air mass dominance, well below May climatological averages of 22°C, as cloud cover limits diurnal heating. Key differentiators include potential afternoon clearing or model tweaks in evening updates; new MGM and global model runs expected overnight could sharpen the 12°C vs. 13°C+ split before resolution via official observations.

markets.question: Will Forsen beat xQc's Minecraft speedrun record by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Forsen breaks xQc's In-Game-Time of 14:27.035 in a Minecraft speedrun during a live stream by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve to “No” as soon as it becomes impossible for Forsen to complete a qualifying speedrun within this market’s timeframe.

For the purpose of this market, “Minecraft speedrun” refers to a playthrough of Minecraft Java Edition version 1.16.1, using a random seed, starting from world creation and ending when the Ender Dragon is defeated.

For the purpose of this market, “Stream” refers to a live video broadcast hosted by Forsen or where Forsen is present. Prerecorded videos, videos not intended for public release, or other non-live content will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Forsen's Twitch channel (https://www.twitch.tv/forsen); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Google Gemini be #2 Free App in the US Apple App Store on May 5?

markets.question: Club Always Ready vs. CA Lanús: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Copa Libertadores game between Club Always Ready and CA Lanús, scheduled for May 5 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Club Always Ready and CA Lanús combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on conmebollibertadores.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Club Always Ready vs. CA Lanús - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Copa Libertadores game, scheduled for May 5 at 8:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Club Always Ready welcomes CA Lanús to Estadio Municipal El Alto at 4,100 meters altitude for their Copa Libertadores Group G return fixture on May 5, where the hosts' extreme elevation routinely disrupts visiting teams' stamina and cohesion. Always Ready, Bolivian Primera División frontrunners in 2nd place with a potent early-season attack (4 goals scored, 0 conceded), absorbed a narrow 0-1 home loss to LDU Quito last week despite controlling possession. Lanús, Copa Sudamericana 2025 champions holding mid-table in Argentine Primera División (around 5th-8th), drew 0-0 at home versus Platense on April 1 amid midfield injuries to R. Loaiza (muscle) and doubts over W. Bou (knee) and D. Aquino (hamstring). The April 16 first leg in Buenos Aires looms large for group standings and momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in Lagos' May 5 high temperature, with forecasts from NiMet-aligned models and sites like Weather.com and AccuWeather clustering around 91°F (33°C) under partly cloudy skies and southwest sea breezes at 7-13 mph. Yesterday's (May 3) observed peak of 34°C—verified by NiMet stations—alongside the agency's seasonal outlook for above-normal May temperatures from persistent warm air masses, nudges odds toward 34°C (30%) over 33°C (27%) and 32°C (20%). Differentiating factors include morning cloud breaks allowing peak solar heating versus afternoon convection or stronger sea breeze moderation, with 17-25% isolated thunderstorm risk capping extremes; final GFS/ECMWF updates today may shift positioning.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 81.5% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, reflecting stalled progress since the company's confidential S-1 filing in January 2026, which targeted a Q1 debut with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan as underwriters but yielded no public registration or pricing details amid lagging monetization. Discord's estimated $800 million annual recurring revenue trails its 200 million monthly active users, constrained by a gamer-centric base reluctant to pay premium fees, contrasting enterprise peers like Slack's $2.5 billion revenue at higher multiples. Private valuation lingers around $7-10 billion, down from $15 billion peaks, vulnerable to SaaS compression; a formal S-1 disclosure remains the key catalyst to shift these market-implied odds.

markets.question: Will China GDP growth in Q2 2026 be between 4.0% and 4.3%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to China's Y/Y Growth Rate (%) of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP" release for Q2 of 2026, scheduled for July 16, 2026.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

For the full release schedule, see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/202512/t20251226_1962154.html

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 100-119 Truth Social posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: With 39 original Truth Social posts verified through May 3—peaking at 22 on May 1 amid endorsements and policy announcements on fishing permits and EU tariffs, dropping to 4 on May 2 with jabs at Bill Maher and Gavin Newsom, rebounding to 13 on May 3 addressing Rudy Giuliani's hospitalization and Iran tensions, and quiet so far on May 4—trader consensus clusters tightly around 100-119 (34%) and 120-139 (27.5%) totals for the May 1-8 window. This reflects President Trump's variable daily cadence, averaging near 19 posts in April's 560 total but prone to overnight sprees or lulls tied to news cycles and schedule. Remaining four days hinge on rallies, international developments, or White House events to push toward higher bins, while subdued periods could favor 80-99.

markets.question: Will Giannis Antetokounmpo play for the Atlanta Hawks in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team Giannis Antetokounmpo officially joins by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo does not officially join a new team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Milwaukee Bucks”.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo retires or is not under contract with any professional team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official acquisition announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the Milwaukee Bucks and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

markets.question: Will Jonas Wind be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Goals scored in Bundesliga matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., DFB-Pokal, German Super Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Bundesliga season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane's commanding 33-goal tally in 31 Bundesliga matches has locked in trader consensus at 99.4% for him as top goalscorer, with Bayern Munich atop the table and just two matchdays remaining after his latest strike in a May 2 win over Heidenheim. Deniz Undav trails at 18 goals for Stuttgart, followed by Patrik Schick (16, Leverkusen) and Luis Díaz (15, Bayern), rendering mathematical catch-up impossible even with hat-tricks in finales. Kane's consistent finishing, Bayern's dominance in fixtures, and no injury concerns solidify his position; only a severe Kane injury sidelining him entirely could theoretically open the door, though his depth in the squad mitigates that risk.

markets.question: Will Trump speak to Pope Leo XIV in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual speaks with Donald Trump between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Speaking is defined as any verbal interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be credible media reporting. However, in the absence of definitive or in the case of contradictory media reporting, statements by either of the specified individuals or their official representatives will also be considered.

Statements by the specified individuals or their official representatives will only be considered conclusive if the claims explicitly, or in context unambiguously, indicate that a qualifying talk occurred within the specified timeframe and took place via verbal communication, and provided that such claims are not contradicted by the other specified individual, their representatives, or credible media reporting by the end of the third calendar date (ET) following the statement in question.

If the date/time of a qualifying talk cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day following this market's above-specified timeframe, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether it was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: White House confirmation of a bilateral summit between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 14-15, rescheduled from March amid U.S. military operations in Iran, has propelled trader consensus to 96% odds on that verbal interaction, bolstered by a U.S. C-17 arrival in China on May 2 for preparations and senior official calls on May 1 covering trade stability, Taiwan, and de-escalation. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte follows at 79% amid alliance discussions on troop adjustments, while Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman trades at 63% tied to Middle East ceasefire diplomacy. Vladimir Putin's 48% reflects an April 29 call on Ukraine and Iran awaiting May follow-up; lower probabilities for Zelenskyy (44%) hinge on potential peace talks, with Taiwan tensions or regional crises posing minor risks to high-probability outcomes before May 31 resolution.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy, Team Spirit's academy roster with VILBy, mazay, Kiryasoo, kidofpain, and s1nside, entered Exort Series #25 Main Stage as a top seed after strong prior showings, dominating Round 1 with a 2-1 BO3 win over Wave Esports on Dust II and Ancient while dropping Mirage. Wave, featuring Katalic, sheezy, miksozzz, and Belof, qualified via 3-1 Contenders Stage run but faltered in Swiss (0-3 overall), exposing map weaknesses like poor Mirage winrate (17%). No prior head-to-head; Spirit advanced to grand final (lost 0-2 to BET-M 33 on Apr 26), highlighting superior adaptability in current CS2 meta amid no roster changes or patch shifts since.

markets.question: Will the U.S. flu hospitalization rate per 100,000 in Week 17 be between 95 and 100?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the cumulative influenza-associated hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the United States, as reported for the specified week.

If the recorded data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be CDC FluView / FluSurv-NET (see: https://www.cdc.gov/fluview/index.html). If the FluSurv-NET hospitalization rate for the specified week is not released by 11:59 PM ET on the tenth calendar day following the date of the prior FluView weekly report release, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

Note: Only the CDC FluSurv-NET cumulative hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the specified week will qualify, regardless of estimates, projections, state-level reports, or other influenza surveillance metrics published by the CDC or other sources.

events.title: Flu Hospitalization Rate Week 17, 2026?
events.description:

Note: Only the CDC FluSurv-NET cumulative hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the specified week will qualify, regardless of estimates, projections, state-level reports, or other influenza surveillance metrics published by the CDC or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: The latest CDC FluView report for Week 16 (ending April 25, 2026) shows the cumulative laboratory-confirmed influenza hospitalization rate at 85.7 per 100,000 population via FluSurv-NET surveillance—up slightly from 85.2 the prior week—marking the third-highest level since 2010-2011 amid a moderate-severity 2025-2026 season dominated by influenza A(H3N2) earlier and rising B strains lately. With national flu activity now decreasing (3.8% clinical lab positivity, weekly rate 0.4 per 100,000), trader consensus overwhelmingly backs 85–90 as Week 17's (ending May 2) cumulative rate stabilizes near current levels. Revisions from reporting delays or an unforeseen late-season surge could push it toward 90+, but low outpatient and emergency trends make this unlikely per official nowcasting. Next FluView expected soon.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Norwegian Nobel Committee's April 30 announcement of 287 nominees—208 individuals and 79 organizations—has fueled a highly fragmented trader consensus for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, with Yulia Navalnaya leading at 7.5% implied probability ahead of Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy at 6.5% each. Navalnaya's frontrunner status stems from her inheritance of Alexei Navalny's anti-authoritarian campaign narrative, amplified by betting site buzz. Trump's momentum arises from reported nominations by leaders in Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan, plus U.K. bookmaker favoritism tied to his diplomatic overtures, while Zelenskyy's bid leverages Ukraine's resilient resistance storyline, as formally nominated alongside its people. With no dominant storyline and historical upsets common, key swing factors include summer lobbying and the October 9 announcement, keeping the race wide open.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 11°C on May 5?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (LOW) $4.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Ayanna Pressley (D), a member of the progressive Squad with deep Boston roots, anchors trader consensus at 93% for Democratic Party victory in MA-07, reflecting the district's entrenched D+33 partisan lean from recent presidential cycles and her landslide past wins exceeding 80% amid urban, diverse voter blocs in Roxbury, Dorchester, and Fenway. No credible Republican challengers have emerged ahead of the September 1 primaries, with Pressley's December 2025 reelection announcement—forgoing a Senate bid—and April endorsements from College Democrats solidifying her position absent polling or disruptions. Upsets could arise from a late GOP heavyweight recruit, national midterm wave favoring Republicans, primary surprise, or unforeseen scandal, though structural barriers remain high through November 3 general election.

markets.question: Everton FC vs. Manchester City FC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Everton FC and Manchester City FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

City dominate head-to-head with 21 recent wins to Everton's six, though Toffees drew 1-1 earlier this season.

markets.question: Will Encore Medical’s market cap be between $50M and $55M at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Encore Medical’s market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on April 22 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before June 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency.

It is calculated as the total number of outstanding shares, multiplied by the official closing share price of the publicly traded class on the first trading day.

If necessary, to accurately capture the company’s total market capitalization, rather than a stock-class-specific market capitalization, the calculation will include all outstanding share classes and apply any stated conversion ratios to the publicly traded class. Where no conversion right exists, such shares will be counted at their stated outstanding amount without discount, unless official filings explicitly specify differently.

The number of outstanding shares will be determined from official company filings or disclosures (e.g., SEC filings). The closing share price on the first trading day will be determined from the primary exchange’s official listing page.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official company filings and the primary exchange’s official listing page. The market capitalization will be determined through appropriate calculation using the total outstanding shares and the closing price from the first day of trading.

In the event of an interruption in the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that day as the first day of trading for the purposes of this market.

markets.question: LoL: KT Rolster vs BNK FEARX - Game 2 Winner

events.title: LoL: KT Rolster vs BNK FEARX (BO3) - Esports World Cup Korea Qualifier Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between KT Rolster and BNK FEARX in the Esports World Cup Korea Qualifier Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 5 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "KT Rolster" if KT Rolster win the match against BNK FEARX.

This market will resolve to "BNK FEARX" if BNK FEARX win the match against KT Rolster.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: KT Rolster holds a commanding edge entering the Esports World Cup 2026 Korea Qualifier Round 1 BO3 against BNK FEARX, anchored by their second-place LCK Rounds 1-2 standing at 8-2 series record (16-6 maps) despite back-to-back losses to Hanwha Life Esports and HANJIN BRION in late April. Their April 24 sweep of FEARX underscores a lopsided head-to-head (9-2 overall), bolstered by a stable roster featuring PerfecT, Cuzz, Bdd, Aiming, and Effort excelling in macro play and draft phases. FEARX sits eighth at 3-7, buoyed by a May 3 win over DN SOOPers but hampered by fresh bot-lane flux with Taeyoon's April 30 addition potentially disrupting synergy in this double-elimination playoff for EWC slots.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chengdu be 27°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/chengdu/ZUUU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This follows May 3's cooler 23°C high under overcast conditions, marking a sharp warming trend with enhanced solar insolation. Differentiating factors include afternoon cloud development—potentially capping at 29°C if increasing—or sustained clear skies pushing to 31°C, against early-May climatological averages of 26°C where ±2°C variability is common due to boundary layer mixing and urban effects. Monitor intraday CMA observations for resolution shifts.
