markets.question: Iran agrees to surrender enriched uranium stockpile by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran publicly agrees to surrender its enriched uranium stockpile by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Iran to surrender its enriched uranium stockpile will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the U.S. or Israel.

An agreement by Iran to surrender any amount of its enriched uranium stockpile will count.

To qualify, Iran must publicly agree that its enriched uranium stockpile, or any portion thereof, will be transferred, shipped, or placed under the custody or control of any entity outside of Iran and its influence, excluding non-state armed groups or Iranian-aligned organizations (such as Hezbollah, the Houthis, or similar actors).

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Iran to surrender its enriched uranium stockpile as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal.

Agreements to merely limit or cap the level or quality of enrichment—such as reducing enrichment to below weapons-grade thresholds—will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran nuclear negotiations remain deadlocked despite President Trump's April 17, 2026, claim that Tehran agreed to surrender its enriched uranium stockpile—over 9 tons per the IAEA's February report—in exchange for $20 billion in frozen assets and sanctions relief. Iranian officials have not confirmed the deal, insisting on their right to uranium enrichment while levels remain negotiable. US-Israeli airstrikes on April 25 damaged key sites like Natanz and Fordow but left the stockpile intact, heightening tensions. Traders watch IAEA verification inspections, the upcoming Board of Governors session, and talks in Oman for progress on a potential 20-year enrichment freeze or breakdown risking escalation.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $285 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Ron DeSantis join the Trump administration by June 30?
markets.description:

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed or nominated by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

Nominations will qualify even if the individual is not confirmed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Thune endures conservative pushback—including calls from Rep. John Rose to oust him—over stalled SAVE America Act voter integrity provisions and a recent funding bill omitting ICE border funding, despite pledges for floor action sans 60-vote filibuster. Summer primaries, filibuster challenges, and polling shifts in swing states could decisively separate frontrunners.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 420-439 tweets from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 1 12:00 PM ET to May 8, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US commercial crude oil inventories plunged 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24—the largest drawdown in recent months—fueled by record exports that positioned the US as a net crude exporter, per the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report. This contrasts with modest builds or stability in prior weeks, where stockpiles hovered around 460-465 million barrels amid steady domestic production near 13 million barrels per day and refinery utilization above 90%. The EIA releases weekly updates every Wednesday, with the next on May 6 covering the prior Friday's data, continuing through June 3 ahead of the June 5 resolution benchmark. Summer driving season demand ramp-up post-Memorial Day, export trends, import levels, and global factors like OPEC+ output decisions could drive further draws or rebuilds, with Cushing hub stocks also monitored for delivery constraints.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

This market will resolve as soon as the relevant chart is published. If the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week is not published within 14 calendar days of the expected release date, this market will resolve to “Other”.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Austin be between 52-53°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/austin/KAUS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts confirm sunny skies over Austin today, May 4, with highs near 86°F at key sites like Austin-Bergstrom International Airport, driving near-unanimous trader consensus on 66°F or higher amid real-money wagers. Current midday observations already surpass 76°F under light southerly winds and clear conditions, bolstered by high-pressure ridging and model agreement from GFS and ECMWF ensembles showing peak diurnally heated temperatures well into the 80s. This aligns with early May climatology, where average highs reach 84°F. Realistic challenges—a sudden influx of marine stratus or northerly wind shift—remain improbable per upper-air data, with official daily summaries expected by evening to resolve the market.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

No general election polling exists yet, but forecasters rate it Safe Democratic. Upsets would require a Biss scandal, extraordinary Republican national midterm wave, or depressed Democratic turnout ahead of the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Brazzaville: Matyas Cerny vs Bor Artnak
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Matyas Cerny and Bor Artnak in the Brazzaville, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Matyas Cerny' if Matyas Cerny advances against Bor Artnak.

This market will resolve to 'Bor Artnak' if Bor Artnak advances against Matyas Cerny.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating US-Cuba tensions from President Trump's May 1 Florida remarks teasing a post-Iran military "takeover" of Cuba via carrier deployment, coupled with mid-April Pentagon reports of ramped-up contingency planning, have pushed trader consensus to a narrow 56% implied probability of no military clash in 2026. Cuban President Díaz-Canel's May 3 denunciation of these as "unprecedented" threats, amid US sanctions and an energy blockade worsening Havana's crisis, underscores risks, yet ongoing diplomacy—including late April US visits to Havana proposing reforms in exchange for prisoner releases—signals potential off-ramps. Senate rejection of curbs on Trump military options adds uncertainty, but focus on Iran operations and historical aversion to invasion keep "No" leading narrowly.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (LOW) $116 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Airbnb (ABNB) shares trade around $142, up 12% over the past month and near the 52-week high of $147, fueled by optimism ahead of the Q1 2026 earnings release on May 7. Analyst consensus rates the stock overweight with an average 12-month price target of $150, reflecting expectations for continued travel demand recovery and AI-driven innovations. Q1 guidance of $2.59–$2.63 billion revenue (14–16% growth) sets the bar; outperformance or upbeat Q2 outlook could propel shares higher by May's end, while softening occupancy or macro headwinds pose risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tigers shortstop Kevin McGonigle leads AL Rookie of the Year trader consensus at 38.5% implied probability, edging White Sox first baseman Munetaka Murakami's 36%, as both deliver standout early-season performances through 30 games. McGonigle tops AL rookies in batting average (.333), on-base percentage (.420), slugging (.518), hits, doubles, and triples, bolstered by a 12-game hit streak and 25 consecutive games reaching base, giving him a WAR edge around 2.0. Murakami counters with explosive power, tying the MLB home run lead at 11—including a three-run shot on May 1—and a 1.020 OPS despite a .256 average, fueling recent odds flips amid voter appeal for counting stats like homers and RBI. The bunched odds reflect traders' split on well-rounded contact skills versus headline-grabbing slugging in a deep rookie class, with momentum hinging on sustained form, injuries, and schedule strength.

She declined to nominate a mandatar on April 25 amid uncertain vote counts, but trader consensus prices Janša at nearly 89% due to his center-right bloc's demonstrated strength, including a late-April National Assembly vote passing an SDS-proposed reduction of ministries from 19 to 14 with 49 supporting votes, nearing the 46-seat threshold for investiture. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If SpaceX is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's confidential SEC filing on April 1, 2026, has fueled trader optimism for a near-term initial public offering, with reports indicating a roadshow kickoff the week of June 8 and potential listing around Elon Musk's June 28 birthday at a staggering $1.75–2 trillion valuation driven by Starlink's surging satellite broadband revenue. This marks a shift from prior delays tied to revenue predictability and Mars milestones, now linked to Musk's compensation incentives for colonization progress. Competitive dominance in reusable rocketry and Starship advancements bolster sentiment, though SEC review timelines and market conditions could introduce slips, as seen in past high-profile tech IPOs. Traders eye the upcoming roadshow for pricing signals amid volatile space sector dynamics.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price an 78.5% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the April 29 FOMC decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% amid persistent but contained inflation pressures. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year on a 10.9% energy surge, yet Chair Powell affirmed anchored inflation expectations with no tightening imperative, while March unemployment held at 4.3% signaling labor market resilience without overheating. CME FedWatch indicates 83.8% odds of unchanged rates through year-end, underscoring skin-in-the-game consensus against hikes. Watch April CPI on May 12 and the June 17-18 FOMC for potential shifts.

markets.question: Spread: Club Independiente Petrolero (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Copa Sudamericana game, scheduled for April 15 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Club Independiente Petrolero" if Club Independiente Petrolero win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Caracas FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on conmebol.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.description: More markets for the Copa Sudamericana game, scheduled for April 15 at 6:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Caracas FC hosts Club Independiente Petrolero in the Copa Sudamericana group stage opener on April 15 at Estadio Olímpico de la UCV, where home advantage could prove key amid Caracas's struggles in Liga FUTVE Apertura—sitting 11th with a 1-4-3 record, including recent draws like 1-1 versus Monagas and losses exposing defensive frailties. Independiente Petrolero, mid-table in Bolivia's Primera División with mixed results such as a recent 0-0 stalemate against Guabirá, brings solid away form but faces travel and adaptation challenges. No major injuries or suspensions reported for either side, and limited head-to-head history leaves the matchup closely contested based on trader consensus, with group positioning implications heightening stakes.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Kostyantynivka by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Kostyantynivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.528896° N, 37.702514° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Kostyantynivka will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of the specified area, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/p9N3w6yRVfjx6cuZ8

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have reached the outskirts of Kostyantynivka, a key logistical hub in Donetsk Oblast's fortress belt defending Kramatorsk and Sloviansk, using infiltration tactics amid intensified assaults over the past week. Ukrainian defenders report repelling persistent attacks, with geolocated footage and ISW assessments from late April confirming no confirmed advances into the town despite heavy artillery and drone strikes. Following stalled progress near Pokrovsk, trader sentiment reflects uncertainty over Russian manpower sustainability and Ukrainian reinforcement capabilities, as clashes in the sector logged over 20 daily engagements recently. Upcoming aid deliveries and frontline rotations could tip the balance before the market's resolution date per ISW maps.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming Copa Libertadores game, scheduled for Thursday, May 7, 2026 between CA Platense and CA Peñarol.

eventMetadata.context_description: CA Platense enters as trader consensus slight favorite at 46% implied probability for their Copa Libertadores Group E home clash against Peñarol, buoyed by a 2-1 upset victory in the first leg at Montevideo that vaulted them to second in the standings behind Corinthians. Peñarol's recent domestic losses and mounting injury crisis—key doubts over Ferreira, Darias, and Ángulo, plus long-term absences like Leonardo Fernández (knee) and Nahuel Herrera (shoulder)—have eroded their away threat to 24%, while conserving players for this pivotal fixture underscores its stakes for top-two qualification. Platense plans a near-identical lineup to their winning XI, with only minor tweaks like Gómez for Amarfil, amplifying home form in a closely contested matchup where draw pricing at 30.5% reflects defensive resilience on both sides.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 77.5% implied probability for Lady Gaga attending tonight's Met Gala, driven by the stark absence of any official confirmation from her team or organizers despite persistent rumors. Early April reports from Vanity Fair and industry insiders speculated her return after a seven-year hiatus to promote her role in The Devil Wears Prada 2, but no verified statements or sightings have materialized in the critical 48 hours leading up to the event—only ambiguous social media nostalgia posts from stylist Frederic Aspiras and makeup artist Sarah Tanno on May 3, plus a May 2 paparazzi photo of her en route to New York City. Her selective red carpet history and packed post-Mayhem Ball commitments underscore the high uncertainty, with resolution hinging on real-time red carpet reports starting at 5:30 p.m. ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the wide-open NY-12 Democratic primary to replace retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler, trader consensus gives Assemblyman Micah Lasher a slim 40.5% edge over Assemblymember Alex Bores at 38.5%, with Jack Schlossberg at 22%, reflecting a fluid race amid high undecideds shown in recent polls. Bores' mid-March internal survey (released April 17) placed Schlossberg first at 22%, Bores second at 19%, and Lasher third at 14% with 28% undecided, but Lasher surged on markets following Gov. Kathy Hochul's April 13 endorsement—his second major establishment backing after Bloomberg—signaling potential consolidation among party insiders. Mid-April candidate forums at 92nd Street Y and elsewhere amplified scrutiny, keeping the contest tight; independent polls, fundraising disclosures, or additional endorsements could tip the balance before the June 23 vote.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 16°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward a 19°C high in Madrid on May 5 at 41% implied probability, narrowly ahead of 20°C at 35.5%, despite the latest AEMET forecast projecting 18°C under partly cloudy skies with 55% precipitation odds and southwesterly winds at 20 km/h. This reflects ensemble model spreads from ECMWF and GFS, which show variability around 18–20°C amid a recent cooling trend from early May peaks near 21°C, triggered by a weakening high-pressure ridge allowing Atlantic moisture advection and persistent cloud cover to limit insolation. Differentiating factors include afternoon shower timing—prolonged overcast favors 18–19°C, while breaks in clouds could boost heating to 20°C. Watch AEMET updates and ECMWF 12Z runs today, with resolution via Barajas Airport observations.

markets.question: Will Nationalist Party win the most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

markets.question: Will Le Mans FC achieve promotion from Ligue 2 to Ligue 1 for the 2026-27 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team achieves promotion from the French Ligue 2 to Ligue 1 for the 2026–27 season at the conclusion of the current league season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in Ligue 1 for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Ligue 2, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by the Ligue 1 and Ligue 2.

If the 2025-26 Ligue 2 season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from Ligue 1 and Ligue 2. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: ESTAC Troyes has clinched automatic promotion to Ligue 1 as Ligue 2 champions with 67 points from 33 matches, capping a dominant season marked by 20 wins and a +28 goal difference, confirmed after recent victories solidified their lead. Le Mans holds second on 59 points, but Saint-Étienne and Red Star FC—both on 57—loom large with favorable goal differences (+16 and +22) heading into the final matchday on May 8, where results could secure the second direct spot or shuffle playoff seeding. Rodez AF (55 points) and Reims (53) battle for 3rd-6th promotion playoff berths, whose winner earns the third Ligue 1 place in mid-May knockouts; head-to-head form and home advantage in finales will prove decisive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's landslide victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention last weekend, capturing over 70% of delegate votes for the party's endorsement and securing automatic primary ballot access, has solidified trader consensus around his 83% implied probability as the GOP nominee for governor. The event narrowed the field, qualifying Brian Shortsleeve—who garnered enough support to advance but now resists calls to exit amid Minogue's momentum—while Mike Kennealy fell short of the 15% threshold needed, relegating him to a long-shot signature drive. Minogue's outsider appeal as a biotech executive and strong fundraising further bolster his frontrunner status ahead of the September 1 primary, reflecting party delegates' preference for his platform amid criticism of state leadership.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Cape Town be 19°C on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Cape Town International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Cape Town International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/za/matroosfontein/FACT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The South African Weather Service's 06:00 SAST forecast for May 4 projects a Cape Town maximum temperature of 18°C under cloudy skies with isolated to scattered showers and rain—particularly widespread in southwestern parts—driving trader consensus to 44% implied probability for 18°C, narrowly ahead of 19°C at 39.5%. This reflects the eastward shift of heavy downpours and severe systems over the past 48 hours, ushering cooler westerly to northwesterly winds (moderate to fresh, strengthening briefly) that cap solar insolation and daytime heating below the early-May climatological average of 20-21°C. ECMWF, GFS ensembles, and AccuWeather align on a tight 18-19°C spread, with uncertainty hinging on afternoon cloud persistence versus partial clearing. Current METARs at Cape Town International Airport report 15°C amid light rain and 100% humidity; monitor SAWS's 16:00 update and hourly observations for resolution-defining shifts.

Late April Glengariff Group (April 25) and Emerson College surveys show U.S. Rep. John James leading 37%-20% and tying 20%-21% respectively, reflecting James's name recognition from prior U.S. Senate runs, though undecideds exceed 30%. Aric Nesbitt polls at 3-7% but garners 15% trader support via Senate minority leader role. Tom Leonard's April 23 withdrawal could consolidate votes for top contenders in this open-seat race succeeding term-limited Gov. Gretchen Whitmer.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, with 95% of votes counted showing a margin just under 20 points over Republican Joe Hathaway, drives trader consensus toward the 20-25% victory bucket at overwhelming odds. The D+5 district, vacated by Gov. Mikie Sherrill, favored Mejia after her progressive upset in the February Democratic primary and pre-election polls indicating a double-digit edge, amplified by Democratic overperformance relative to 2024 presidential results. Late provisional ballots and vote-by-mail counts in late April further boosted her in Passaic County, solidifying the gap. While final certification could adjust tallies, a GOP surge in outstanding ballots would be needed to drop below 20%, an unlikely shift given current trends.

markets.question: Will Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Analyst price targets average $400+, implying modest upside, though macroeconomic pressures like Treasury yields and competition in AI could influence May's trading range. markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding victory in the 2026 Miami Grand Prix on May 3—his third win from four races, including poles in Japan and Miami—has propelled him to a 20-point drivers' championship lead on 100 points, fueling trader consensus at 39.1% implied probability for the title. Teammate George Russell's consistent podium finishes, including second in Australia and strong Miami recovery to extend Mercedes' constructors' lead to 180 points over Ferrari's 110, underpin his 26.5% standing despite qualifying penalties. Lando Norris' Miami Sprint win boosts his 11.5% at 51 points, but McLaren's 94 points trail, while Red Bull's struggles drop Max Verstappen to 4.5% on just 26 points amid poor race pace and DNF risks. Early-season Mercedes superiority drives the market, though 19 remaining Grands Prix leave room for challengers.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 35-39 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Recent US intelligence reports detailing severe injuries to successor Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, including limited official access, have fueled expectations of subdued activity, contrasting the new @MKhamenei_ir account's May 1 cluster of nine posts marking Workers' and Teachers' Days. Earlier April markets resolved around 10-14 posts, but three weeks without updates signal trader caution on resumption.

markets.question: Will annual inflation increase by ≤3.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about inflation over the 12-month period ending April 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

This market will resolve to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending in April 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release, which reports inflation over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g., 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices April 2026 CPI year-over-year inflation near 3.7%, with 37% implied probability for exactly 3.7% edging out 3.8% at 30%, reflecting a tight contest driven by March's hotter-than-expected 3.3% print—up sharply from February's 2.4% amid a 10.9% gasoline surge tied to Middle East tensions. Sticky shelter costs and base effects from last year's low readings sustain elevated expectations, while potential energy moderation offers downside risk for 3.6% (17%). Core CPI dynamics and volatile commodities differentiate outcomes, ahead of the Bureau of Labor Statistics release on May 12, which could sway Federal Reserve rate path views amid funds steady at 3.50%-3.75%.

Early April 2026 headline CPI eased to 4.53% year-on-year from prior highs, signaling modest disinflation that supports a potential pause after the expected May 7 rate reduction priced at over 90% in parallel markets. Governor remarks on March 30 highlighted the easing cycle nearing completion, with core price pressures and rising global yields reinforcing trader caution for further adjustments. The May decision remains the pivotal catalyst ahead.

markets.question: O/U 1.5 Rounds
markets.description:
Each market will resolve to “Over” if the fight between Yaroslav Amosov and Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, lasts beyond the listed round threshold.

Each market will resolve to “Under” if the fight between Yaroslav Amosov and Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, does not last beyond the listed round threshold.

For example:

“Over 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight continues past the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

“Under 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight ends before the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

If the fight ends exactly at the threshold (e.g., 2:30 of Round 2 for a 1.5 line), it will resolve “50-50.”

If the bout is not scored, postponed beyond May 23, 2026, or canceled, all options will resolve “50-50.”

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC 328: Yaroslav Amosov vs. Joel Álvarez (Welterweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yaroslav Amosov" if Yaroslav Amosov is officially declared the winner of the fight against Joel Álvarez at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026.

It will resolve to "Joel Álvarez" if Joel Álvarez is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yaroslav Amosov enters his second UFC bout as the welterweight prelims focal point against Joel Álvarez at UFC 328, where trader consensus has shifted sharply toward the former Bellator champion's elite wrestling pedigree following his dominant first-round anaconda choke submission of Neil Magny in December 2025. Álvarez, riding a four-fight win streak capped by a unanimous decision over Vicente Luque last October, brings 6'3" reach and lethal bottom-game submissions but faces stylistic peril against Amosov's relentless takedown chains and ground control at 170 pounds. No injury concerns reported ahead of Friday's weigh-ins, heightening focus on Álvarez's takedown defense versus Amosov's pressure in this closely contested matchup.

Bryce Harper trails closely at 26.3% with a strong power surge, posting six homers and 19 RBI alongside an .832 OPS through late April, leveraging Phillies' lineup protection. Mookie Betts holds 25.4% despite a sluggish .179 average in limited action, buoyed by Dodgers' offensive depth and career excellence. Juan Soto's 14.0% reflects his Mets transition and .314 early average with .915 OPS, while Ronald Acuña Jr.'s 9.7% anticipates rebound from an ACL recovery amid a .252 start and career-low strikeouts. Small-sample volatility tempers current NL batting leaders like Ildemaro Vargas.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors FC Barcelona at 53.5% implied probability in this La Liga El Clásico at Camp Nou, driven by their commanding league lead—top with 79 points from a 26-1-4 record and +54 goal difference—bolstered by a recent five-match winning streak. Real Madrid trails at 70 points amid a defensive injury crisis, including long-term absences for Éder Militão and Arda Güler, plus recent hamstring issues sidelining Kylian Mbappé until late May, weakening their attack significantly. Barcelona's home form and healthier squad contrast Madrid's inconsistent results post-injuries like Aurélien Tchouaméni's overload, positioning the draw at 22.5% and Madrid at 25% as viable but uphill outcomes in a tightly contested title race.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 72.5% implied probability against the U.S. enacting an AI safety bill before 2027, driven by persistent congressional gridlock despite recent bipartisan momentum. The White House's March 20 National AI Legislative Framework urged light-touch federal rules to preempt patchwork state laws, but no comprehensive safety measures have advanced past introduction amid partisan divides on regulatory scope. Late April saw bills like the American Leadership in AI Act and Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act filed, focusing on standards, child safety, and transparency, yet none have cleared committees. With states like Nebraska and Maryland passing targeted AI regulations and the 119th Congress facing lame-duck uncertainties post-midterms, traders anticipate delays into 2027 or beyond.

markets.question: Will Amazon have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - May 4, 6:40AM-6:45AM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Jeddah be 34°C on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sa/jeddah/OEJN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest short-range forecasts from global models including ECMWF and GFS indicate Jeddah's highest temperature on May 4 will peak around 37-38°C under a dominant subtropical high-pressure ridge fostering subsidence, clear skies, and intense solar heating, driving the market's 73% implied probability for 39°C or higher as traders factor in frequent model underestimations during May's climatological peak season. Coastal influences from warming Red Sea surface temperatures and urban heat island effects in densely populated Jeddah often push observed highs 1-2°C above guidance, with historical May data showing daily maxima averaging 37.2°C but exceeding 39°C on over 30% of days. Monitor National Center for Meteorology updates and King Abdulaziz Airport observations through afternoon for real-time shifts, as sea breeze onset could cap intensification.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2026 WNBA championship following contract extensions for core stars Breanna Stewart and Sabrina Ionescu, ensuring roster continuity after their 2024 title and strong 2025 Finals push, with recent re-signings like Jonquel Jones bolstering frontcourt depth amid training camp. Indiana Fever sit second at 16% on rising momentum from Caitlin Clark and Aliyah Boston's dynamic duo, plus Commissioner's Cup win and solid free agency additions around their young core. Defending champions Las Vegas Aces, fresh off sweeping Phoenix Mercury for their third crown in four years with A'ja Wilson re-signed supermax, trail slightly at 15.5% as competition intensifies league-wide post-expansion. Atlanta Dream's 11% reflects retaining starters Rhyne Howard, Allisha Gray and Jordin Canada, differentiating via backcourt retention in a wide-open field with new entrants like Toronto Tempo and Portland Fire. Preseason tilts, including Fever's win over Liberty, underscore the parity.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's SpaceX IPO market cap strongly backs a closing valuation over $2 trillion at 55.5% implied probability, driven by the company's confidential SEC filing on April 1 and outlined early June roadshow targeting a late-June listing near Elon Musk's birthday. Starlink's confirmed revenue surge to $4.4 billion annually—up 842% in two years—with 9 million subscribers and forecasts of $18-25 billion in 2026 from high-margin satellite broadband has ignited hype, elevating expectations far above recent $350 billion private tender valuations. Reusable Falcon and Starship dominance in launches, plus NASA contracts, fuel the premium, tempered by xAI capital burn risks. Key catalysts include roadshow details and final pricing for this potential record-breaking debut.

markets.question: Spread: CA Platense (-1.5)
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "CA Platense" if CA Platense win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Estudiantes de La Plata".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaprofesional.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Estudiantes de La Plata hold second place in the Liga Profesional Apertura table with an impressive defensive record, conceding only nine goals across 10 matches, bolstering trader consensus for their away challenge at Platense's Estadio Ciudad de Vicente López on May 3. Platense sit 10th amid a mid-table campaign hampered by injuries to key defender Gonzalo Goñi (Achilles tendon rupture, out until August), Iván Gómez (hamstring), Héctor Bobadilla (bruise), and others like Franco Minerva (strain). Estudiantes also miss Santiago Arzamendia (cruciate ligament tear) and José Sosa (Achilles issues), but their form edges the matchup. The sides' prior March fixture was postponed, following Estudiantes' 2-1 Trofeo de Campeones win in December.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 160-179 Truth Social posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Atletico's momentum has stalled after a heartbreaking Copa del Rey final penalty-shootout loss to Real Sociedad on April 18, where key forwards Ademola Lookman and Alexander Sorloth sustained injuries, casting doubt on their fitness for the first leg at the Metropolitano on April 29 and exposing defensive vulnerabilities. Leading the Premier League table, Arsenal contend with Martin Odegaard's ongoing knee issue and Bukayo Saka's ankle doubt ahead of their April 25 Newcastle clash, while Atletico sit fourth in La Liga amid a form dip—traders weigh home-soil resilience against Arsenal's attacking firepower and head-to-head edge.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro at 63% to remain Venezuela's official head of state by December 31, 2026, due to persistent institutional recognition despite his January 3 U.S. military capture and ongoing trial. Venezuela's Supreme Court appointed Vice President Delcy Rodríguez as interim leader, but market rules specify that temporary measures do not transfer the presidency, with UN-recognized sources continuing to identify Maduro. Recent developments include Rodríguez's power consolidation through purges of Maduro allies, U.S. sanctions relief boosting oil exports, and stalled democratic transition with no elections scheduled; opposition figure María Corina Machado remains sidelined, planning a late-year return amid frozen-out status. Upcoming judicial reforms could influence formal recognition.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Mets' dismal 9-19 start, capped by a humiliating sweep at home by the Colorado Rockies over the April 24-26 series—including a rain-postponed doubleheader—has traders weighing persistent injury woes and offensive struggles. Star shortstop Francisco Lindor landed on the 10-day IL with a left calf strain requiring at least three weeks out, while Kodai Senga joined the 15-day IL for lumbar inflammation after a poor outing, and Jorge Polanco nursed a wrist contusion. The Rockies (around 11-16) capitalized despite Willi Castro's day-to-day knee soreness, snapping Mets' momentum amid their 14 losses in 16 games. Historical Mets dominance in head-to-heads faltered at Citi Field, highlighting bullpen vulnerabilities and lineup depth issues as key sentiment drivers.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Jiujiang: Cody Wong vs Xinxin Yao
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Cody Wong and Xinxin Yao in the Jiujiang, originally scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 1:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Cody Wong' if Cody Wong advances against Xinxin Yao.

This market will resolve to 'Xinxin Yao' if Xinxin Yao advances against Cody Wong.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 200-219 tweets from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 5 12:00 PM ET to May 12, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.
