markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hungary's parliamentary election on April 12 delivered a landslide defeat to Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party, with Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party securing a supermajority in the National Assembly and prompting Orbán's immediate concession after 16 years in power. Orbán subsequently relinquished his parliamentary seat on April 25 and offered to resign as Fidesz leader on April 28, though the party deferred the decision, solidifying trader consensus at 97.2% that he will exit office before 2027 amid smooth transition talks expected by mid-May. Minimal odds for peers like Netanyahu, Starmer, or Zelenskyy reflect stable incumbencies without recent no-confidence votes, snap elections, or parliamentary losses. Barring an unprecedented failure to form the new government or Orbán mounting a comeback via snap polls, his departure remains the dominant outcome.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Korea increase the base rate after the May Meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the base rate resulting from the Bank of Korea’s May monetary policy meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Korea after its May 28, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official Bank of Korea meeting schedule: https://www.bok.or.kr/eng/bbs/E0000627/view.do?nttId=10094301&searchCnd=1&searchKwd=&depth2=400417&depth3=400022&depth=400022&pageUnit=10&pageIndex=1&programType=newsDataEng&menuNo=400022&oldMenuNo=400022

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Korea's policy statement for their May 28, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the base rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 94.5% implied probability of no change in the Bank of Korea's base rate at its May 28 monetary policy decision, reflecting the board's seventh straight hold at 2.50% in April amid stable inflation—March CPI rose modestly to 2.2% year-over-year—and robust Q1 GDP growth prompting upward revisions to 2.1–3% forecasts that diminish easing expectations. This positioning aligns with the U.S. Federal Reserve's recent pause, prioritizing data-dependent caution under new Governor Shin Hyun-song. Hawkish remarks today from Deputy Governor Ryoo Sang-dai, citing rising inflation risks and won depreciation to 17-year lows near 1,480 per dollar, underscore potential tightening, though a surprise surge in tomorrow's April CPI release or further currency weakness could challenge the hold consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Ilia Topuria as the frontrunner at 60.5% implied probability to claim UFC pound-for-pound #1 status in 2026, driven by his undefeated run including knockout title wins over Max Holloway and Charles Oliveira before moving to lightweight, with a high-profile defense against Justin Gaethje slated for late June at UFC's White House event. Petr Yan's 43% follows his thrilling decision reclaim of the bantamweight title from Merab Dvalishvili late last year, though back surgery in January has sidelined him until summer, tempering momentum. Khamzat Chimaev (40.1%), the dominant middleweight champion after submitting Dricus du Plessis, faces Sean Strickland in a pivotal title defense on May 9 at UFC 328, while Du Plessis (36.4%) eyes a comeback rematch amid recent form questions. Islam Makhachev holds current #1 with welterweight gold, supporting the 33% "Islam/None" option amid no major ranking shifts post recent Fight Nights.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Mexico City be 21°C on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Benito Juárez International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Benito Juárez International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/mx/mexico-city/MMMX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Servicio Meteorológico Nacional's latest forecast projects maximum temperatures of 30-35°C across northern Mexico City amid an ongoing heatwave driven by high-pressure systems and minimal cloud cover, underpinning the 99.3% trader consensus for 26°C or higher today. Current model ensembles from SMN and international sources like NOAA align on peak afternoon warmth around 2-5 PM local time, exceeding May climatological averages of 25-27°C at the city's 2,240-meter elevation. While inherent forecast uncertainty persists—such as potential isolated showers capping peaks—scenarios challenging this include widespread convective activity or northerly winds introducing cooler air masses, with real-time observations from Benito Juárez Airport stations providing final resolution by midnight.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices EUR/USD around 1.173 as of May 4, 2026, buoyed by Eurozone inflation surging to 3% in April—the highest since September 2023—despite Q1 GDP expanding a tepid 0.1% quarter-over-quarter, below expectations. ECB's April 30 decision to hold key rates steady masks hawkish undertones, with markets implying 50 basis points of tightening per IMF scenarios, potentially steepening yield differentials against a steady Fed funds rate. Geopolitical risks, including Middle East tensions bolstering the dollar, cap upside. Watch June ECB meeting, US nonfarm payrolls on May 7, and FOMC dot plots for shifts in growth and policy divergence driving year-end resolution.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Yaroslav Amosov enters his second UFC bout as the welterweight prelims focal point against Joel Álvarez at UFC 328, where trader consensus has shifted sharply toward the former Bellator champion's elite wrestling pedigree following his dominant first-round anaconda choke submission of Neil Magny in December 2025. Álvarez, riding a four-fight win streak capped by a unanimous decision over Vicente Luque last October, brings 6'3" reach and lethal bottom-game submissions but faces stylistic peril against Amosov's relentless takedown chains and ground control at 170 pounds. No injury concerns reported ahead of Friday's weigh-ins, heightening focus on Álvarez's takedown defense versus Amosov's pressure in this closely contested matchup.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation remains tightly contested, with market-implied odds favoring 2.7–2.9% at 44.8% narrowly ahead of 2.4–2.6% at 40.1%, reflecting balanced views on persistent mid-2% pressures amid stabilizing price data. March 2026 headline CPI rose to 2.2% year-over-year—below the 2.4% consensus—but core inflation held at 2.2%, while Bank of Korea signals mid-to-high 2% outcomes and IMF projections hit 2.5%, buoyed by Middle East tensions elevating energy costs and April CPI forecasts near 2.6%. Key differentiators include oil price volatility and Q1 GDP strength supporting demand; the April CPI release on May 6 could tip the scales ahead of the next BoK meeting.

markets.question: Spread: Cádiz CF (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 8 at 2:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Cádiz CF" if Cádiz CF win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "RC Deportivo La Coruña".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Cádiz CF vs. RC Deportivo La Coruña - More Markets

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Deportivo La Coruña, entrenched in second place in LaLiga Hypermotion standings with 64 points from a strong run of wins and draws—including a 3-1 victory over Mirandés last weekend and a 1-1 draw at Burgos on April 25—enter Jornada 39 as promotion contenders against relegation-battling Cádiz CF, mired in 18th on 38 points. Cádiz's winless streak over five matches features heavy losses like 3-0 to Sporting Gijón and 3-0 at Real Valladolid, exacerbated by injuries to key attackers Javi Ontiveros (groin), Suso (muscle), and Bojan Kovacevic (knee). The earlier season 2-2 draw at Riazor highlights Cádiz's home fightback potential, but Deportivo's away form and table position drive trader sentiment toward the visitors.

markets.question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $400 on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on May 4 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of India (Marxist) (CPI(M)) win the most seats in the 2026 Kerala Legislative Assembly election?
markets.description:
Legislative Assembly elections are to be scheduled to be held in Kerala, India, in March–May 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Kerala Legislative Assembly in the 2026 election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. If that also results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Kerala Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (https://eci.gov.in). If multiple official reports differ, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the greatest number of Assembly Constituencies (ACs).

eventMetadata.context_description: Counting completed on May 4 for Kerala's April 9 Legislative Assembly election reveals a resounding victory for the Indian National Congress (INC)-led United Democratic Front (UDF), capturing 129 of 140 seats and forming the majority government, with INC securing 59 seats as the single largest party. This rout of the incumbent Left Democratic Front (LDF), led by CPI(M) with just 23 seats, stems from decade-long anti-incumbency fueled by governance critiques, validated by pre-election exit polls forecasting UDF dominance and local body trends signaling LDF erosion. BJP-led NDA managed only 4 seats. Trader consensus at 99.1% for INC reflects irreversible vote tallies, though improbable post-poll legal challenges or recounts could theoretically alter certification.

markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open until September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET to allow for confirmation. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects negligible risk to major banks like BNY Mellon and JPMorgan Chase failing by June 30, with implied probabilities under 5%, amid resilient capital positions at systemically important institutions despite commercial real estate (CRE) headwinds. Two small regional failures marked 2026—the latest, Community Bank and Trust-West Georgia on May 1 ($288 million assets), driven by nonperforming loans over 10% from aggressive lending, following Metropolitan Capital Bank & Trust's January 30 closure ($261 million assets) linked to CRE losses in skilled nursing facilities. Approximately $875 billion in CRE loans mature through year-end, stressing 59 regionals with exposures exceeding three times Tier 1 capital per public filings, though FDIC deposit insurance limits contagion. Key catalysts include the June FOMC meeting on potential rate cuts to ease net interest margin pressures and Q1 earnings disclosures revealing credit quality trends.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures (CL) hover near $102 per barrel amid heightened geopolitical risk premiums from Iran-related disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which have spurred a recent surge above $100 despite a symbolic OPEC+ output hike of 206,000 barrels per day for May. Last week's EIA report revealed a larger-than-expected 6.2 million barrel commercial inventory draw, tightening near-term supply balances and countering softer monthly price trends down 9.5%. Summer driving season demand and weekly inventory releases through June remain key catalysts, alongside potential Middle East escalations or China economic data influencing global consumption; traders weigh these against recession risks curbing upside.

markets.question: Will Juan Branco win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

Édouard Philippe at 20.5% reflects his surge as the strongest center-right challenger, with polls like Harris Interactive indicating he edges Bardella in runoffs, appealing to moderate voters wary of extremes. The tight race persists due to splintered fields—no-confidence dynamics post-2024 snap elections, left-wing disunity under Mélenchon (9%), and pending primaries like Les Républicains' Retailleau endorsement—while candidate declarations or endorsements before April 2027 voting could consolidate blocs and widen leads.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Félix Tshisekedi remains firmly entrenched midway through his second five-year term, constitutionally secured until December 2028 after his 2023 re-election victory, driving trader consensus to a 91% implied probability against his removal by year-end 2026. Recent developments, including his March 2026 decree establishing a specialized economic criminal court and February meetings with UN Secretary-General and AFRICOM leadership, underscore ongoing governance initiatives and international engagement amid eastern DRC's persistent M23 rebel conflict. Ruling coalition pushes for constitutional amendments enabling a third term have advanced via parliamentary purges, signaling confidence despite diaspora protests and opposition criticism over security and corruption. Absent major escalations like a successful coup, health crisis, or impeachment, traders see low near-term ouster risk.

markets.question: Will Danny Musovski win the 2026 MLS Golden Boot?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Golden Boot.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Petar Musa's 10th goal in FC Dallas' latest match vaulted him atop the MLS 2026 Golden Boot table after roughly 10 matchdays, one clear of Sam Surridge's blistering 9 goals in 7 games for Nashville SC, fueling trader consensus on their upset potential despite trailing defending champion Lionel Messi's 41.5% implied probability. Messi sits seventh with 7 goals in 9 appearances for Inter Miami CF—highest-rated player per advanced metrics—while Denis Bouanga lags at 4 for LAFC yet commands 38.5% on his prior top-three finishes and recent Player of the Matchday honors. With 24-plus games remaining, no major injuries, favorable schedules for frontrunners, and challengers like Hugo Cuypers (8 in 6) and Brian White (8 in 9) lurking, the race stays tightly contested.

markets.question: Will Lovable be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors AI coding platform Cursor and gaming giant Caesars Entertainment as leading acquisition targets before year-end 2026, driven by concrete catalysts in the surging AI and M&A landscapes. Cursor's probabilities surged following SpaceX's April 21 announcement of a major partnership, including a $60 billion acquisition option that explicitly qualifies under market rules, amid hyperscalers' race for developer tools and talent previously eyed by Microsoft. Caesars faces ongoing takeover talks from Tilman Fertitta's Fertitta Entertainment since March, potentially valuing it at $6.5-7 billion. Viking Therapeutics ranks third on big pharma pursuit of its obesity drug pipeline. Regulatory hurdles and lofty AI valuations cap enthusiasm, with Q2 developer conferences and option deadlines as key watchpoints.

markets.question: Obras vs. San Lorenzo
markets.description:
In the upcoming LNB game, scheduled for February 12 at 6:30PM ET:
If the Obras win, the market will resolve to "Obras".
If the San Lorenzo win, the market will resolve to "San Lorenzo".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Obras Sanitarias holds a slim 51.5% implied probability edge in this LNB matchup, driven by their recent 76-70 home win over San Lorenzo on April 18 at Microestadio Luis Conde and subsequent 95-89 road victory against Instituto in the reclasificación playoffs, underscoring strong recent form and defensive resilience. The competitive balance reflects San Lorenzo's historical championship pedigree despite a mid-table 37% win rate (15th in standings), with mixed results like losses to Olimpico, countered by Obras' three straight head-to-head triumphs. Home-court advantage bolsters Obras, but San Lorenzo's talent could rebound; late injury reports, lineup confirmations, or rest from tight playoff scheduling could tip trader consensus either way.

markets.question: Will Croatia advance through the first Eurovision Semi-Final?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" for the listed countries whose candidates for Eurovision 2026 advance according to the results of the Eurovision First Semi-Final, scheduled for May 12, 2026.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to advance from the Eurovision 2026 First Semi-Final based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this competition is not held or no contestants advance by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of the First Semi-Final for Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: With rehearsals underway at Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle ahead of the May 12 first semi-final, early impressions from openers like Moldova's high-energy Satoshi ("Viva, Moldova!") and frontrunners Finland's Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen ("Liekinheitin"), Greece's Akylas ("Ferto"), and Sweden's Felicia ("My System") have solidified trader consensus around Nordic and Balkan powerhouses, reflecting their dominant national final wins and polished stagings. The April running order positions these acts favorably mid-show, boosting implied qualification odds, while bubble contenders like Georgia, Poland, and Montenegro eye televote surges amid competitive previews. Jury deliberations and full dress rehearsals through May 10 could shift dynamics before 10 spots lock for the May 16 grand final.
